# City of Red Vale Ordinance No. 2024-17
## Establishing the Department of Building Permits and Inspections

**Adopted by the City Council of the City of Red Vale on June 18, 2024**

### Section 1. Findings and Purpose

The City Council finds that the protection of life, health, property, accessibility, structural integrity, and orderly development requires a dedicated municipal department with clear authority to administer construction permitting, plan review, inspection, occupancy approval, complaint investigation, and code enforcement functions.

The purpose of this ordinance is to establish the Department of Building Permits and Inspections as a permanent department of the City of Red Vale and to define its authority, structure, duties, and powers.

### Section 2. Establishment

There is hereby established a department of the municipal government to be known as the **Department of Building Permits and Inspections**, hereafter “the Department” or “DBPI.”

The Department shall be an executive department of the City of Red Vale and shall report through the City Manager unless otherwise provided by charter or subsequent ordinance.

### Section 3. Jurisdiction

The Department shall exercise its authority within the territorial jurisdiction of the City of Red Vale and, where authorized by intergovernmental agreement, within any additional service areas delegated to the City.

### Section 4. Core Functions

The Department shall administer and perform the following functions:

1. receive, review, route, and act upon applications for permits and related approvals;
2. review plans, specifications, deferred submittals, revisions, and supporting materials for compliance with applicable law;
3. issue permits, approvals, corrections, notices, certificates, and other official determinations within its jurisdiction;
4. schedule and conduct field inspections and record the results of those inspections;
5. investigate complaints and suspected violations of building, housing, property maintenance, accessibility, fire-life-safety coordination matters, and related municipal code requirements within its delegated authority;
6. issue notices of correction, notices of violation, stop-work directives, unsafe condition notices, and other enforcement documents authorized by law;
7. coordinate with planning, zoning, fire prevention, public works, environmental health, finance, legal, and records offices as needed to carry out its responsibilities;
8. maintain official records relating to permits, inspections, violations, hearings, appeals, fees, payments, and final dispositions; and
9. provide public information, service counters, scheduling support, and records access consistent with law and adopted policy.

### Section 5. Powers

In carrying out its functions, the Department is authorized to:

1. adopt internal procedures, bulletins, checklists, and administrative guidance consistent with law;
2. require applications, plans, calculations, reports, affidavits, certifications, and supporting documents reasonably necessary for review;
3. determine whether a submission is complete for intake and review purposes;
4. condition, approve, deny, suspend, revoke, or close permits and related approvals as authorized by applicable law;
5. enter property at reasonable times and in lawful manner for inspection and investigation as authorized by law;
6. require correction of unlawful, unsafe, unpermitted, or nonconforming work;
7. order work stopped where immediate continuation would violate law or create unsafe conditions;
8. assess or refer for assessment such fees, penalties, and administrative charges as authorized by ordinance or resolution;
9. refer matters for administrative hearing, civil enforcement, or legal action where voluntary compliance is not achieved; and
10. certify final completion, occupancy authorization, or case closure when applicable requirements have been satisfied.

### Section 6. Department Director

The Department shall be headed by a **Director of Building Permits and Inspections**, appointed in accordance with the personnel and charter provisions of the City of Red Vale.

The Director shall:

- supervise the operations of the Department;
- assign or delegate duties to divisions, officers, and employees;
- issue written administrative interpretations consistent with adopted law;
- prepare budgets, staffing plans, and performance reports;
- coordinate with other agencies on cross-jurisdictional matters; and
- serve as the Department’s accountable executive for official records and policy implementation.

### Section 7. Divisions and Units

The Director may organize the Department into divisions and units including, but not limited to:

- Permit Services Division
- Plan Review Division
- Field Inspections Division
- Code Enforcement Division
- Records and Disclosure Unit
- Appeals and Hearings Liaison
- Business Operations Unit

The creation or modification of internal units shall not alter the substantive authority granted by this ordinance.

### Section 8. Official Records

The Department shall maintain official records of applications, permits, plans, revisions, inspections, findings, notices, enforcement actions, appeals, hearings, fees, payments, certificates, and final dispositions. Department records shall be retained, disclosed, and disposed of according to law, the citywide retention schedule, and approved records management policy.

### Section 9. Public Service Obligations

The Department shall publish service information sufficient for applicants, property owners, contractors, and the public to understand:

- available services and eligibility;
- required forms and supporting materials;
- service standards and scheduling procedures;
- fees and payment requirements;
- correction and appeal processes; and
- methods for filing complaints and requesting records.

### Section 10. Due Process and Appeals

No final adverse enforcement action subject to hearing shall be imposed except in the manner provided by applicable ordinance, rule, or law. The Department shall provide notice of the basis for action, available cure periods where applicable, and appeal rights when required.

Nothing in this section shall prevent immediate action authorized by law to address dangerous or unsafe conditions.

### Section 11. Fees

Fees for permits, inspections, reinspections, records services, and other Department functions shall be established by separate fee resolution or ordinance. The Department shall not waive mandatory fees except as specifically authorized by law or policy.

### Section 12. Interagency Coordination

Where a matter implicates zoning, fire prevention, environmental health, public works, utilities, finance, or legal enforcement, the Department may require referral, consultation, concurrence, or parallel review before final action.

### Section 13. Severability

If any section, subsection, sentence, clause, or phrase of this ordinance is held invalid, the remaining portions shall continue in full force and effect.

### Section 14. Effective Date

This ordinance shall take effect thirty days after adoption.

### Section 15. Codification

The City Clerk is directed to codify this ordinance within the Red Vale Municipal Code as **Title 15, Chapter 15.04 — Department of Building Permits and Inspections**.
